narrative: Markets rallied sharply for the week as a temporary U.S.–Iran ceasefire and partial reopening of the Strait of Hormuz eased energy-supply fears and sparked a broad risk-on move, especially in tech and consumer discretionary. March CPI printed hot on headline but broadly met expectations, keeping the focus on geopolitics and the oil-price unwind into the weekend talks.

value_picks.name: Salesforce, Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Salesforce hit a 52-week low amid sector rotation away from tech and consulting slowdown fears, trading at depressed multiples despite strong FCF generation and recent dividend initiation.
value_picks.bull_case: AI integrations and enterprise CRM dominance position it for recovery as IT budgets stabilize; $25B buyback supports shareholder value.

value_picks.name: Accenture plc
value_picks.why_undervalued: Accenture reached 52-week lows on weak guidance and consulting sector headwinds, but offers attractive valuation with robust balance sheet.
value_picks.bull_case: Diversified services and cloud migration tailwinds will drive rebound; consistent dividend growth adds appeal.

value_picks.name: Intuit Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Near 52-week low due to tax season cyclicality and competition concerns, undervalued relative to growth in fintech.
value_picks.bull_case: TurboTax and QuickBooks dominance ensures steady cash flow; AI enhancements boost margins.

value_picks.name: Adobe Inc.
value_picks.why_undervalued: Creative Cloud slowdown fears pushed it to 52w low, but subscription model provides visibility.
value_picks.bull_case: AI features like Firefly drive adoption; recurring revenue model supports long-term growth.

value_picks.name: Abbott Laboratories
value_picks.why_undervalued: Healthcare sector pressures and patent issues led to 52w low, but diversified portfolio resilient.
value_picks.bull_case: Nutraceuticals and diagnostics growth offset; strong pipeline for recovery.

momentum_picks.name: MaxLinear, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Q1 earnings beat with 43% YoY revenue growth driven by AI data center optical products; raised FY guidance and multiple analyst upgrades.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: High momentum in AI infra but volatile semis sector.

momentum_picks.name: Skillz Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Breakout in gaming platform amid mobile esports surge and partnerships.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Speculative small cap with high beta to consumer trends.

momentum_picks.name: Intel Corporation
momentum_picks.catalyst: Semiconductor rebound and foundry progress amid AI chip demand.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Turnaround story with execution risks but undervalued assets.

momentum_picks.name: Aehr Test Systems, Inc.
momentum_picks.catalyst: Wafer test demand for SiC and AI chips; strong quarterly results.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Cyclical but positioned in EV/AI growth.

momentum_picks.name: Credo Technology Group
momentum_picks.catalyst: Optical DSP ramps for hyperscalers in AI infrastructure.
momentum_picks.risk_reward: Premium valuation vulnerable to capex slowdowns.

trends: U.S.–Iran ceasefire drove a relief rally as crude oil fell sharply, reversing prior energy-led inflation fears.
Mega-cap tech and semis reasserted leadership as investors rotated back into AI/compute beneficiaries amid improved risk appetite.
Small caps outperformed on a risk-on impulse and easing input-cost concerns as energy prices retreated.
Energy sector lagged the broader market given the oil drawdown, while cyclicals and growth led.
Volatility eased as geopolitics shifted from escalation risk to negotiation risk into the weekend meeting.
risks: Ceasefire durability and weekend U.S.–Iran negotiations: renewed conflict could reprice oil and risk assets quickly.
Inflation pass-through from prior energy spike: risk of additional hot prints complicating Fed-cut expectations.
Earnings season kickoff next week: guidance sensitivity to energy, demand, and AI capex could drive dispersion.
Treasury supply/demand and rate volatility: auction digestion remains a key cross-asset variable.
Crowded positioning in AI/semis: sharp reversals possible if headlines turn or yields back up.
